item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My only question is: who the (REDACTED) allowed testing on children, and who are these children.

Personal opinion here, but I can totally see the Foundation allowing testing on children if the anomaly is repeatedly confirmed to be harmless. SCP-348 has a test log involving schoolchildren who are told that the soup is part of a trial run of new cafeteria food.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Short and sweet, but I feel like I've read a hundred "short and sweet" articles on this site. At this point, I'm somewhat burnt out on them, and this one doesn't do enough to set itself apart.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is awesome, weird, and metal. My only question is: who the (REDACTED) allowed testing on children, and who are these children.
Why am I the only one worried about this

item.title: Re: sweet dreams ♪
item.content:encoded: Reminds me much of G!Sans (Gaster Sans) from EchoTale. Love the concept and can't wait to read more!!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This has a lot of things going for it, not the least of which being that it has an excellent balance between specifics and mystery. I think it's the perfect length as it is (although it is paragraphed a little weirdly), and I especially love how much foreshadowing and buildup it manages to pack into such a small space. It's such a rollercoaster for being so short.

On 10 May 2018, SCP-3892-01 looked directly into a testing chamber camera after kissing the subject, growled, and said, "I know where you sleep." SCP-3892-01 then loudly cracked each of its knuckles and demanifested as normal.

This part in particular, I think, speaks volumes, and is a great use of redaction. If the comments are anything to go by, we all had just about the same impression.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: In before someone writes a tale about MC&D where Mr. Dark complains about Etsy running them out of business.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
why
why this
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This one was on Etsy.

"CHILD'S BLANKET, COMES WITH FLAMING SKELETON WARRIOR (guaranteed non-hostile)"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ya reckon it's a trucker skeleton? I totally imagine a bikie skeleton fella.

I'm down for a crossover though!

(also, +1)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm assuming this is related to SCP-3899?

Quality work as always, FuelTank. Now I want to be tucked in by the ghost of Joey Fucknuts.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this recent rise of short and simple skips.

+1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Metal.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I get that it's short n sweet, I prefer longer SCP articles and I know that's just my personal preference. I don't think it's asking for much, I just get curious about where the Foundation finds these anomalous items. ^^

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's not just that, it's the fact that this is perfect as it is.

This has exactly what it needs. It establishes the premise then delivers a punchline. Adding more just weighs it down with unnecessary details.

And even then, this has one of my favorite things in an article: a hint of a story. It's (imo) one the perfect examples of a series I-esque article done right. It gives you something to work with, leaves the surrounding details up to you. It's a little view into the weird, anomalous world the Foundation operates in.

(To cadavarcommander: all my +1, an excellent job once again.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, not with that attitude.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the scp, I just have one problem. Skeletons don't have lips.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was thinking of BACA, too. :) That final threat really makes the article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Aww! Nurturing metal bedtime demon! Love it. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
See? Not all spooky folks are bad folks! Some happen to be bikers who like to tuck kids into bed!

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Metal skeleton biker parent is best parent.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
God dammit, Cad. Stop writing good things :v

+1 for cute and funny biker man who is angry at cameras

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't quite understand how I guessed that you had written this one…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I personally feel the exact opposite, I think that would totally ruin it. This SCP is short and sweet on purpose, and all the better for it. Adding in unnecessary extra logs would detract from the simplicity of a BACA skeleton tucking kids in to bed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it, but it feels a bit bare bones, no pun intended. I would have liked to know, how the Foundation found this SCP in the first place. A little backstory to flesh it out, like reports of a fiery spectre in a children's hospital, that scared away nurses and janitors, for peeping on the kids? I mean other than that, this one is a really nice idea.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Meta-analysis of articles ruined this a little to be honest; the fact that this was in the positives made the ending pretty obvious because if it was anything else it would have been downvoted into oblivion, heh.

item.title: Updooted
item.content:encoded: I personally liked the line at the end before the change, though I guess that's just me. Ah well. Still +1ing anyway.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like the lack of notes concerning what happens if the child is woken up after the skeleton kisses them good night.

+1 for fear of being beaten up by heavy metal skeletal parent.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: They're super awesome. Always makes me happy to read stories about super tough biker peoples giving a kid a blanket and standing watch over a house so the kid can sleep peacefully. There's hope in the world.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: BACA. Bikers Against Child Abuse. If I ever get time, I'm gonna look into volunteering with them. Badass group of folks.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A solid read. Makes me think of the biker gangs that look out for abused children and walk with them to keep them safe (BACA, I believe?).

+1 from me here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was listening to Deathcore as I read this in Nic Cage's (as Ghost Rider) voice. This could not have complimented the article perfectly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is a delightfully weird combination. Like Weryllium said, this is so on-brand and off-brand of the usual CadaverCommander skip, and I think the contrasts are what makes it so enjoyable.

Who's the real boogeyman, huh?

In terms of style this one is definitely "you" while also clearly "not-you", which makes for a delicious snack. Great work as usual, CC.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As I continue to fail to fall asleep the night before a final, I happen to come across both the most cozy and metal SCP I’ve read. And the only thing I can think is “fuck I could use this right now”

+1 for making me want to go back to sleep. But like, because you’ve made it sound nice. I realize it sounds weird but I swear it’s a compliment.

item.title: sweet dreams ♪
item.content:encoded:
my fourteenth item.

many thanks to Weryllium for saying that this was fine.

and thank you, reader. good night. ♥

a kind and shiny-new youtuber named Skele Jones has elected to do a reading for us:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Cx1_QROkIx8
this person has a voice like a waterfall of warm molasses and it's certainly worth a listen ♥
